meaning: adj.未被抓伤(或擦伤)的 未被撕裂的
examples: The financial tsunami triggered by the Lehman Brothers left no market unscratched.
This baby, after being buried over 24 hours, miraculously, was rescued unscratched.
He asked several Toronto retailers if they would object to him buying tickets and then exchanging the unused, unscratched tickets.
But for the mirrors, my car made it through almost unscratched.
He had to send an Ontario lottery official a batch of unscratched cards in two stacks, one winning one losing, before getting a suddenly speedy rise out of him.
